description_long: This object is a straight-edged sword with a broad, double-edged blade that tapers to a rounded tip, currently encased in a fitted sheath. The hilt features a prominent crossguard that extends horizontally, providing hand protection, and a cylindrical handle with subtle texturing for grip. The pommel is rounded and slightly flared, capping the end of the handle. The sheath is simple and unadorned, closely following the blade's shape and ending in a slightly pointed tip. The entire object appears to be a uniform dark color, suggesting a utilitarian design with no visible ornamentation. The proportions and construction indicate it is a functional weapon, likely made of metal with a possible synthetic or leather-wrapped handle.
description: A straight, double-edged sword with a crossguard and a simple, dark sheath, featuring a textured cylindrical handle and rounded pommel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the sword in its sheath, with the crossguard and handle clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blade and sheath, with the handle and pommel seen end-on.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the sheath, crossguard, and handle from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile of the sword and sheath, with minimal detail visible.
